This diagnostic is informational.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Replace [WorkflowRunTerminationCondition] with [AgentTerminationCondition] on the group chat member.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGroupChatMember("code-review")]
[WorkflowRunTerminationCondition(typeof(KeywordTerminationCondition), "APPROVED")]
public class ApproverAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGroupChatMember("code-review")]
[AgentTerminationCondition(typeof(KeywordTerminationCondition), "APPROVED")]
public class ApproverAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Suppress if you intentionally want the condition to fire in Layer 2 (e.g. you are also using the raw workflow object in code that bypasses the generated Run*Async() method and need consistent termination semantics).

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF011
[AgentGroupChatMember("code-review")]
[WorkflowRunTerminationCondition(typeof(KeywordTerminationCondition), "APPROVED")]
public class ApproverAgent { }
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF011

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF009 — condition declared on a non-agent class
  • NDLRMAF010 — condition type does not implement IWorkflowTerminationCondition

docs.title: NDLRMAF016: Cycle detected in agent graph
Cause
docs.text: A named agent graph declared via [AgentGraphEdge] attributes contains a cycle.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text:
Agent graph workflows use superstep-based BSP (Bulk Synchronous Parallel) execution, which requires a directed acyclic graph (DAG). A cycle in the graph — where an agent can eventually reach itself through a chain of edges — will cause infinite execution at runtime.

The analyzer performs a topological sort across all [AgentGraphEdge] declarations within each named graph and reports an error when a back-edge is detected.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Break the cycle by removing or redirecting one of the edges that forms the loop.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(CoderAgent))]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(PlannerAgent))] // cycle: Planner → Reviewer → Coder → Planner
public class CoderAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(CoderAgent))]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class CoderAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Do not suppress this diagnostic. Cycles in agent graphs always cause infinite execution at runtime. If you need iterative behaviour, consider using a handoff topology or a group chat with termination conditions instead.

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF016
// Not recommended — cycles cause infinite execution
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF016

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF017 — graph has no entry point
  • NDLRMAF022 — graph contains unreachable agents

docs.title: NDLRMAF017: Graph has no entry point
Cause
docs.text: A named agent graph has [AgentGraphEdge] declarations but no [AgentGraphEntry] to identify the starting agent.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: Every named agent graph requires exactly one [AgentGraphEntry] declaration to identify the starting agent. Without an entry point, the source generator cannot emit a factory method for the graph workflow. The analyzer detects graphs that have edge declarations but no corresponding entry point.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Add [AgentGraphEntry] to the agent class that should be the starting point of the graph.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { } // no [AgentGraphEntry]

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Do not suppress this diagnostic. A graph without an entry point cannot be executed and no workflow factory method will be generated.

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF017
// Not recommended — graph will not be generated
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF017

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF018 — graph has multiple entry points
  • NDLRMAF021 — graph entry point is not a declared agent

docs.title: NDLRMAF018: Graph has multiple entry points
Cause
docs.text: A named agent graph has more than one [AgentGraphEntry] declaration.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: Each named agent graph must have exactly one [AgentGraphEntry]. Multiple entry points create ambiguity about where execution begins — the source generator cannot determine which agent should receive the initial input. Remove the extra [AgentGraphEntry] attributes so only one remains for the graph name.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Remove [AgentGraphEntry] from all but one agent in the graph.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")] // first entry point
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(CoderAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")] // second entry point — error
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(CoderAgent))]
public class DesignerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class CoderAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(CoderAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(CoderAgent))]
public class DesignerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class CoderAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Do not suppress this diagnostic. Multiple entry points in a single graph are always a topology error.

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF018
// Not recommended — ambiguous graph entry
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF018

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF017 — graph has no entry point
  • NDLRMAF021 — graph entry point is not a declared agent

docs.title: NDLRMAF019: Graph edge target is not a declared agent
Cause
docs.text: An [AgentGraphEdge] references a target type that is not decorated with [NeedlrAiAgent].

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: Types used as targets in [AgentGraphEdge] must be decorated with [NeedlrAiAgent] so Needlr can register and resolve them at runtime. Without the agent declaration, the target type will not be discovered by the source generator and the graph will fail to resolve the agent at runtime.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Add [NeedlrAiAgent] to the target type, or remove it from [AgentGraphEdge] if it was referenced by mistake.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

// Missing [NeedlrAiAgent]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Do not suppress this diagnostic. The graph will fail at runtime if an edge target is not a registered agent.

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF019
// Not recommended — graph edge will fail to resolve
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF019

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF020 — graph edge source is not a declared agent
  • NDLRMAF001 — handoff target is not a declared agent (analogous rule for handoff topology)

docs.title: NDLRMAF020: Graph edge source is not a declared agent
Cause
docs.text: A class has [AgentGraphEdge] but is not itself decorated with [NeedlrAiAgent].

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: The class that carries [AgentGraphEdge] is a node in the agent graph and must be registered with Needlr via [NeedlrAiAgent]. Without the agent declaration, the source class will not be part of any generated workflow and the edge declaration is meaningless.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Add [NeedlrAiAgent] to the class, or remove [AgentGraphEdge] if it was added by mistake.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
// Missing [NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Suppress if the [AgentGraphEdge] is intentionally on a non-agent class for metadata purposes (uncommon).

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF020
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF020

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF019 — graph edge target is not a declared agent
  • NDLRMAF003 — handoff source is not a declared agent (analogous rule for handoff topology)

docs.title: NDLRMAF021: Graph entry point is not a declared agent
Cause
docs.text: A class has [AgentGraphEntry] but is not itself decorated with [NeedlrAiAgent].

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: The class that carries [AgentGraphEntry] is the starting node of the graph workflow and must be registered with Needlr via [NeedlrAiAgent]. Without the agent declaration, the entry point class will not be discovered by the source generator and the graph workflow cannot be created.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Add [NeedlrAiAgent] to the class, or remove [AgentGraphEntry] if it was added by mistake.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
// Missing [NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Suppress if the [AgentGraphEntry] is intentionally on a non-agent class for metadata purposes (uncommon).

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF021
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
public class PlannerAgent { }
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF021

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF017 — graph has no entry point
  • NDLRMAF020 — graph edge source is not a declared agent

docs.title: Cause
docs.text: An agent that declares [AgentGraphEdge] for a named graph is not reachable from that graph's [AgentGraphEntry] point via any path.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: Every agent in a named graph must be reachable from the entry point through a chain of [AgentGraphEdge] declarations. An unreachable agent will never be executed at runtime — its edges are wasted declarations. This usually means the agent was added to the wrong graph or a connecting edge is missing.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Either add an edge from a reachable agent to the unreachable one, move the agent to the correct graph name, or remove its graph edges if it should not participate.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))] // unreachable — no edge leads here
public class QaAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(QaAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class QaAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Suppress if you are intentionally keeping the agent declaration for future use while the graph is under construction.

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF022
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class QaAgent { }
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF022

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF016 — cycle detected in agent graph
  • NDLRMAF017 — graph has no entry point

docs.title: NDLRMAF024: All edges from fan-out node are optional
Cause
docs.text: A fan-out node (a node with multiple outgoing edges) has all edges marked as IsRequired = false.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: When all outgoing edges from a fan-out node have IsRequired = false, every downstream branch is optional. If all optional branches fail at runtime, the downstream nodes receive no input and the graph may produce empty or unexpected results. Consider making at least one edge required to guarantee that the graph produces meaningful output.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Set IsRequired = true on at least one outgoing edge to ensure the graph always has a guaranteed execution path.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent), IsRequired = false)]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(QaAgent), IsRequired = false)]
public class PlannerAgent { } // all edges optional

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class QaAgent { }

docs.title: After
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent), IsRequired = true)]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(QaAgent), IsRequired = false)]
public class PlannerAgent { } // at least one required edge

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class QaAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Suppress if empty results from the fan-out are acceptable for your workflow and you handle the case downstream (e.g. via a fallback or default response).

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF024
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent), IsRequired = false)]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(QaAgent), IsRequired = false)]
public class PlannerAgent { }
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF024

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF016 — cycle detected in agent graph
  • NDLRMAF027 — terminal node has outgoing edges

docs.title: NDLRMAF025: CreateGraphWorkflow is incompatible with GraphJoinMode.WaitAny
Cause
docs.text: Code calls CreateGraphWorkflow("graphName") on a graph that declares [AgentGraphNode(JoinMode = GraphJoinMode.WaitAny)] on one or more nodes.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text:
CreateGraphWorkflow returns a MAF Workflow object that uses Bulk Synchronous Parallel (BSP) execution. BSP uses mandatory synchronization barriers — every superstep waits for ALL active nodes to complete. There is no MAF primitive for "proceed when any upstream node completes."

Calling CreateGraphWorkflow on a graph with WaitAny nodes will throw NotSupportedException at runtime. This analyzer catches the problem at compile time.

Use RunGraphAsync instead — it auto-detects WaitAny nodes and uses Needlr's own graph executor with Task.WhenAny for fan-in points.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Replace CreateGraphWorkflow with RunGraphAsync.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
// WRONG — will throw NotSupportedException at runtime
var workflow = factory.CreateGraphWorkflow("my-graph");
var results = await workflow.RunAsync(question);

docs.title: After
docs.text:
// CORRECT — auto-selects MAF BSP for WaitAll, Needlr executor for WaitAny
var results = await factory.RunGraphAsync("my-graph", question);

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text: Do not suppress this diagnostic. The code will throw NotSupportedException at runtime.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — DAG Graph Workflows
  • ADR-0001: DAG/Graph Workflow Support

docs.title: NDLRMAF027: Terminal node has outgoing edges
Cause
docs.text: A node marked with [AgentGraphNode(IsTerminal = true)] also has outgoing [AgentGraphEdge] declarations.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: A node marked with IsTerminal = true on [AgentGraphNode] is expected to be a leaf node with no outgoing edges. Having both IsTerminal = true and [AgentGraphEdge] declarations is contradictory — the node cannot simultaneously be a terminal endpoint and route to other agents. Either remove IsTerminal = true or remove the outgoing edges.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text: Remove IsTerminal = true if the node should route to other agents, or remove the [AgentGraphEdge] declarations if it should be a terminal node.

docs.title: Before
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)] // marked terminal
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(CoderAgent))] // but has outgoing edge
public class ReviewerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class CoderAgent { }

docs.title: After (option A — remove IsTerminal)
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(CoderAgent))]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class CoderAgent { }

docs.title: After (option B — remove the outgoing edge)
docs.text:
[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphEntry("pipeline")]
[AgentGraphEdge("pipeline", typeof(ReviewerAgent))]
public class PlannerAgent { }

[NeedlrAiAgent]
[AgentGraphNode("pipeline", IsTerminal = true)]
public class ReviewerAgent { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text:
Do not suppress this diagnostic. The combination of terminal flag and outgoing edges is always contradictory.

#pragma warning disable NDLRMAF027
// Not recommended — contradictory node configuration
#pragma warning restore NDLRMAF027

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AI Integrations — Multi-Agent Orchestration
  • NDLRMAF016 — cycle detected in agent graph
  • NDLRMAF024 — all edges from fan-out node are optional

docs.title: NDLRMAF028: Condition method not found or has wrong signature
Cause
docs.text: The Condition property on [AgentGraphEdge] references a method name that does not exist on the declaring class, is not static, does not return bool, or does not accept a single object? parameter.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text:
When an [AgentGraphEdge] specifies a Condition, the runtime invokes that method at execution time to decide whether to follow the edge. If the method name is misspelled, has the wrong signature, or is an instance method, the edge will fail at runtime with an InvalidOperationException.

This analyzer catches these mistakes at compile time.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text:
Ensure the condition method exists on the class that declares the [AgentGraphEdge] attribute and has the correct signature.

// WRONG — method name typo
[AgentGraphEdge("Pipeline", typeof(WorkerAgent), Condition = "ShouldRout")]
public class EntryAgent { }

// WRONG — not static
[AgentGraphEdge("Pipeline", typeof(WorkerAgent), Condition = "ShouldRoute")]
public class EntryAgent
{
    public bool ShouldRoute(object? input) => true;
}

// CORRECT
[AgentGraphEdge("Pipeline", typeof(WorkerAgent), Condition = "ShouldRoute")]
public class EntryAgent
{
    public static bool ShouldRoute(object? input) => true;
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AgentGraphEdgeAttribute
  • ADR-0001: DAG/Graph Workflow Support

docs.title: NDLRMAF029: Reducer method not found or has wrong signature
Cause
docs.text: The ReducerMethod property on [AgentGraphReducer] references a method name that does not exist on the declaring class, is not static, does not return string, or does not accept IReadOnlyList<string>.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text:
A reducer aggregates the outputs from multiple parallel branches into a single input for the downstream node. At runtime, the method is invoked via reflection. If the method name is misspelled or has the wrong signature, the graph will fail at runtime.

This analyzer catches these mistakes at compile time.

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text:
Ensure the reducer method exists on the class that declares the [AgentGraphReducer] attribute and has the correct signature.

// WRONG — method name typo
[AgentGraphReducer("Pipeline", ReducerMethod = "Merg")]
public class MyReducer { }

// WRONG — not static
[AgentGraphReducer("Pipeline", ReducerMethod = "Merge")]
public class MyReducer
{
    public string Merge(IReadOnlyList<string> inputs) => string.Join(", ", inputs);
}

// CORRECT
[AgentGraphReducer("Pipeline", ReducerMethod = "Merge")]
public class MyReducer
{
    public static string Merge(IReadOnlyList<string> inputs) => string.Join(", ", inputs);
}

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• AgentGraphReducerAttribute
  • ADR-0001: DAG/Graph Workflow Support

docs.title: NDLRSIG001: HubPath must be a constant
Cause
docs.text: The hubPath parameter of HubPathAttribute is not a compile-time constant expression.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: SignalR hub paths must be known at compile time for proper route registration and AOT compatibility. Using non-constant values (like static fields or method calls) prevents the source generator from properly analyzing and generating the hub registration code.

docs.title: Incorrect
docs.text:
public static class HubPaths
{
    public static string ChatHub = "/chat"; // Not const!
}

[HubPath(HubPaths.ChatHub, typeof(ChatHub))] // NDLRSIG001
public class ChatHubRegistration : ISignalRHub { }

docs.title: Correct
docs.text:
public static class HubPaths
{
    public const string ChatHub = "/chat"; // const is OK
}

[HubPath(HubPaths.ChatHub, typeof(ChatHub))]
public class ChatHubRegistration : ISignalRHub { }

// Or use a string literal directly
[HubPath("/chat", typeof(ChatHub))]
public class ChatHubRegistration : ISignalRHub { }

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text:
1. Change the hub path to a const string field
  2. Use a string literal directly in the attribute
  3. Use nameof() if deriving the path from a type name

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text: This warning should not be suppressed. Non-constant hub paths will cause compilation errors in the C# compiler itself (CS0182).

docs.title: See Also
docs.text: • Advanced Usage

docs.title: NDLRSIG002: HubType must be a typeof expression
Cause
docs.text: The hubType parameter of HubPathAttribute is not a typeof() expression.

docs.title: Rule Description
docs.text: SignalR hub type references must use the typeof() operator for proper type resolution at compile time. This ensures AOT compatibility and allows the source generator to properly analyze the hub type.

docs.title: Incorrect
docs.text:
[HubPath("/chat", someTypeVariable)] // NDLRSIG002
public class ChatHubRegistration : ISignalRHub { }

docs.title: Correct
docs.text:
[HubPath("/chat", typeof(ChatHub))]
public class ChatHubRegistration : ISignalRHub { }

docs.title: How to Fix
docs.text:
Always use typeof(YourHubClass) for the hub type parameter:

[HubPath("/notifications", typeof(NotificationHub))]
public class NotificationHubRegistration : ISignalRHub { }

docs.title: When to Suppress
docs.text: This warning should not be suppressed. Non-typeof expressions will cause compilation errors in the C# compiler itself (CS0182).

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• Advanced Usage
  • NDLRSIG001: HubPath must be a constant
